file.usage_note: Create an ObservableObject with FileFacet for metadata and ContentDataFacet for hash values

email.usage_note: Model each email as an ObservableObject with EmailMessageFacet. Use separate ObservableObjects with EmailAddressFacet for sender/recipients

network.usage_note: Model network connections as ObservableObjects with NetworkConnectionFacet. Use separate ObservableObjects with IP address facets for endpoints

mobile.usage_note: Model a mobile device as an ObservableObject with MobileDeviceFacet and DeviceFacet. Add SIMCardFacet for SIM-specific data

device.usage_note: Model hardware devices as ObservableObjects with DeviceFacet for manufacturer/model and OperatingSystemFacet for OS details

account.usage_note: Model user accounts as ObservableObjects with AccountFacet. Use DigitalAccountFacet or ApplicationAccountFacet for more specific account types

tool.usage_note: Tool represents the software/hardware instrument used in an action (e.g., Autopsy, EnCase). Do NOT use Tool for evidence items — use ObservableObject instead

message.usage_note: Model messages (SMS, chat) as ObservableObjects with MessageFacet or SMSMessageFacet. Link sender/recipient via separate ObservableObjects

browser.usage_note: Model browser artifacts as ObservableObjects with the appropriate facet: BrowserBookmarkFacet, BrowserCookieFacet, URLFacet, or URLHistoryFacet

location.usage_note: Model physical locations with Location and LatLongCoordinatesFacet for GPS coordinates or SimpleAddressFacet for street addresses

malware.usage_note: Model malware samples as ObservableObjects with FileFacet, ContentDataFacet for hashes, and additional facets for behavioral indicators

image.usage_note: Model image files as ObservableObjects with RasterPictureFacet for dimensions, FileFacet for file metadata, and EXIFFacet for camera/GPS data

disk.usage_note: Model disk images as ObservableObjects with DiskFacet and DiskPartitionFacet. Use FileSystemFacet for file system metadata

process.usage_note: Model running processes as ObservableObjects with ProcessFacet for PID, name, creation time, and parent process references

investigation.usage_note: Model the investigation itself with Investigation. Use InvestigativeAction for each forensic step (acquisition, analysis). Link tools and evidence via the action's properties

hash.usage_note: Store hash values using ContentDataFacet on an ObservableObject. HashFacet contains the method (SHA-256, MD5) and value
